title: Pilosella leptobrachia

intro: Pilosella leptobrachia, also known as Leptobrachia, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella leptobrachia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Pilosella leptobrachia is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and gastrointestinal disorders.
